channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: U.K. inquiry lays bare 'catastrophic' missed chances before stabbings at dance class
item.description: An inquiry found that a mass killing by a British teenager in 2024 at a Taylor Swift-themed dance class could have been prevented if his parents and state agencies had acted on his violence fixation.
item.content:encoded:
An inquiry found that a mass killing by a British teenager in 2024 at a Taylor Swift-themed dance class could have been prevented if his parents and state agencies had acted on his violence fixation.

(Image credit: Scott Heppell)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: After years of speculation, a reporter claims to have uncovered the founder of Bitcoin
item.description: After years of speculation, New York Times reporter John Carreyrou explains why he thinks he identified the true founder of Bitcoin.
item.content:encoded: After years of speculation, New York Times reporter John Carreyrou explains why he thinks he identified the true founder of Bitcoin.
item.dc:creator: Mary Louise Kelly

item.title: A U.S. military blockade of Iran's ports takes effect. What does that mean?
item.description: A U.S. blockade of Iranian ports has gone into effect Monday, while President Trump claims Iran wants to make a deal.
item.content:encoded: A U.S. blockade of Iranian ports has gone into effect Monday, while President Trump claims Iran wants to make a deal.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Northam

item.title: 5 things to know about Péter Magyar, Hungary's new prime minister
item.description: Magyar ended Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's 16-year grip on power in a landslide victory on Sunday. The former Orbán loyalist burst onto the scene as an opposition leader in 2024.
item.content:encoded:
Magyar ended Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's 16-year grip on power in a landslide victory on Sunday. The former Orbán loyalist burst onto the scene as an opposition leader in 2024.

(Image credit: Attila Kisbenedek)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Trump says U.S. will blockade Iranian ports. And, Orbán loses Hungarian election
item.description: President Trump announced a blockade of Iranian ports after peace talks with Iran collapsed. And, Viktor Orbán concedes defeat after 16 years in power in Hungary.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump announced a blockade of Iranian ports after peace talks with Iran collapsed. And, Viktor Orbán concedes defeat after 16 years in power in Hungary.

(Image credit: Attila Kisbenedek)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Trump vows to sink Iranian ships approaching a U.S. blockade of Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump said the U.S. would interdict vessels that had paid what he called an "illegal toll" to Iran to cross the Strait of Hormuz.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump said the U.S. would interdict vessels that had paid what he called an "illegal toll" to Iran to cross the Strait of Hormuz.

(Image credit: Atta Kenare)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Trump says U.S. will blockade Iranian ports after peace talks fail
item.description: On Sunday, President Trump said the U.S. would blockade the Strait of Hormuz after negotiations between the U.S. and Iran broke down over the weekend.
item.content:encoded: On Sunday, President Trump said the U.S. would blockade the Strait of Hormuz after negotiations between the U.S. and Iran broke down over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Greg Myre

item.title: Retired U.S. Navy admiral on Trump's threat to blockade the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin speaks to retired U.S. Navy Adm. James Foggo, dean of the Center for Maritime Strategy, about President Trump's command to blockade the Strait of Hormuz.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin speaks to retired U.S. Navy Adm. James Foggo, dean of the Center for Maritime Strategy, about President Trump's command to blockade the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Pope Leo brushes off Trump criticism amid growing Vatican–U.S. tensions over Iran war
item.description: Pope Leo XIV says he will not be deterred by criticism from President Trump, vowing to continue his calls for peace as tensions escalate between the Vatican and Washington over the Iran conflict.
item.content:encoded:
Pope Leo XIV says he will not be deterred by criticism from President Trump, vowing to continue his calls for peace as tensions escalate between the Vatican and Washington over the Iran conflict.

(Image credit: Andrew Medichini)
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Akinwotu

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: President Trump says U.S. will blockade the Strait of Hormuz, failed U.S.-Iran negotiations cause oil prices to surge, Hungary's Viktor Orbán loses reelection bid.
item.content:encoded: President Trump says U.S. will blockade the Strait of Hormuz, failed U.S.-Iran negotiations cause oil prices to surge, Hungary's Viktor Orbán loses reelection bid.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez
